title: Cardiac Science Powerheart G5 Defibrillator - Ready Indicator Failure or Self-Test Errors
description: Troubleshooting red Ready indicator and self-test failures on the Powerheart G5 AED related to pads, battery, and environment.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm the AED is not in active clinical use. If needed, replace with a known working unit before proceeding.

steps.title: Observe the Ready Indicator and Audible Alerts
steps.instructions: Green = Ready. Red X + periodic beep = failure detected. A red indicator means the device failed a self-test and is not rescue-ready.

steps.title: Check Pad Cartridge Installation and Expiration
steps.instructions: Verify pads are fully seated and connector is secure. Check expiration date on pad packaging. Expired or disconnected pads are a common cause of readiness failure. If corrected and indicator turns green, stop here.

steps.title: Check Battery Status and Installation
steps.instructions: Remove and reseat the Intellisense battery. Inspect contacts for debris or corrosion. Verify battery is within expiration date. Low or improperly seated batteries frequently trigger self-test failures. If resolved, allow self-test to complete and confirm green indicator.

steps.title: Perform a Power Reset (Non-invasive)
steps.instructions: Remove battery. Wait 10–15 seconds. Reinsert battery securely. Allow automatic self-test to run. The device should complete a self-test and update status indicator.

steps.title: Check Environmental Conditions
steps.instructions: Confirm device is stored within proper temperature/humidity range. Excess humidity can cause self-test errors (e.g., EC 501). Environmental exposure can cause the AED to fail internal checks and become non-operational.

steps.title: Enter Diagnostic Mode (If Available)
steps.instructions: Press both softkeys to access diagnostic feedback. Listen for voice prompts or displayed service messages. This helps identify whether the issue is battery, pads, or internal circuitry.

steps.title: Inspect for Physical Damage or Contamination
steps.instructions: Check casing for cracks. Inspect pad port and battery compartment. Look for moisture intrusion. Physical damage can prevent successful self-testing.

steps.title: Confirm Self-Test Completion
steps.instructions: Close lid fully and allow device to complete its automatic test cycle. A successful test returns the indicator to green. The G5 performs daily, weekly, and monthly self-tests evaluating battery, pads, and circuitry.

CCR.Complaint: Staff reported AED beeping with red Ready indicator during routine check.
CCR.Cause: Expired electrode pads caused self-test failure and non-ready status.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced pads and reseated battery; device passed self-test and returned to service with green indicator.

helpfulDetails: Final device status (returned to service or sent out)

title: Cardiac Science Powerheart G5 Defibrillator - Battery Replacement and Recognition Issues
description: Addresses new battery not recognized, low battery alerts after replacement, or Rescue Ready indicator not returning to green.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm the AED is not in active clinical use. If needed, replace with a known working unit before proceeding.

steps.title: Verify Correct Battery Type
steps.instructions: Confirm the installed battery is the correct model for the Powerheart G5. Incorrect or incompatible batteries will not be recognized by the device.

steps.title: Check Battery Installation
steps.instructions: Remove and reinstall the battery firmly until it is fully seated. A partially seated battery may cause intermittent contact or failure to power.

steps.title: Inspect Battery Contacts
steps.instructions: Visually inspect the battery terminals and device contacts for dirt or debris, bent or recessed pins, and corrosion. Clean gently if needed. Poor contact prevents proper communication.

steps.title: Observe Device Response After Insertion
steps.instructions: After installing the battery, listen for voice prompts, check indicator lights, and watch for automatic self-test initiation. A normal response indicates proper battery recognition.

steps.title: Check Rescue Ready Indicator
steps.instructions: Confirm the indicator returns to green after self-test completion. If red persists, the device is detecting a fault (battery, pads, or internal).

steps.title: Verify Battery Expiration and Condition
steps.instructions: Check the battery expiration date and packaging condition. Expired or improperly stored batteries may fail even if unused.

steps.title: Swap with Known Good Battery
steps.instructions: Install a known working battery from another unit (if available). If the device functions normally → original battery is faulty. If issue persists → likely device-side issue.

steps.title: Check Pad Connection
steps.instructions: Ensure pads are properly connected and within expiration. Some self-test failures may appear battery-related but are caused by pads.

steps.title: Allow Self-Test to Complete Fully
steps.instructions: Do not interrupt the device during its automatic self-test cycle. Interruptions may cause temporary faults or incomplete initialization.

CCR.Complaint: AED not recognizing new battery; Rescue Ready indicator remained red after replacement.
CCR.Cause: Faulty replacement battery failed to communicate with device.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced with known good battery; device passed self-test and returned to service.

helpfulDetails: Battery swapped with known good unit

title: Cardiac Science Powerheart G5 Defibrillator - Pad Connection or Pad Expiration Errors
description: Addresses pad not detected, pad expiration alerts, or Rescue Ready indicator faults related to pad connection or electrode condition.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm the AED is not in active clinical use. If needed, replace it with a known working unit before troubleshooting.

steps.title: Check Rescue Ready Indicator
steps.instructions: Observe the status indicator. Green = system ready. Red = fault present. This confirms whether the issue is active or intermittent.

steps.title: Inspect Pad Expiration Date
steps.instructions: Remove the pad cartridge and verify the expiration date. Expired pads will trigger a fault and must be replaced. If expired, install a new, in-date pad set. If resolved, stop here.

steps.title: Verify Pad Cartridge Seating
steps.instructions: Reinsert the pad cartridge firmly into the device. Ensure it clicks or seats fully. A loose connection is a common cause of detection errors. If indicator returns to green, stop here.

steps.title: Inspect Connector and Contacts
steps.instructions: Check the pad connector inside the AED and on the cartridge: look for bent pins, debris, or corrosion. Clean gently if needed (no liquid intrusion). Poor contact can prevent pad recognition.

steps.title: Check for Packaging or Seal Issues
steps.instructions: Ensure pads are fully sealed, not dried out or damaged, and not previously opened. Compromised pads can trigger both expiration and connection faults.

steps.title: Perform a Manual Lid Cycle (Self-Test Trigger)
steps.instructions: Open and close the lid to force a self-test. Listen for prompts and observe indicator. This helps confirm whether the device re-recognizes the pads. If resolved, stop here.

steps.title: Try a Known Good Pad Cartridge
steps.instructions: Install a new or known working pad cartridge. If the issue clears, original pads were faulty. If issue persists, suspect device-side connection issue.

CCR.Complaint: AED showing pad error and red Rescue Ready indicator during routine check.
CCR.Cause: Expired pad cartridge and poor seating in connector bay.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced pads with in-date cartridge and reseated; device passed self-test and returned to service.

helpfulDetails: Final device status (in service or removed)

title: Cardiac Science Powerheart G5 Defibrillator - CPR Feedback Not Functioning
description: Addresses lack of CPR feedback prompts, missing compression guidance, or feedback device not responding during rescue readiness checks.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety
steps.instructions: Confirm the AED is not in active clinical use. If needed, replace with a known working unit before troubleshooting.

steps.title: Verify CPR Feedback Feature Availability
steps.instructions: Confirm this unit is equipped with CPR feedback capability (e.g., IntelliSense CPR Device). Some configurations do not include this feature.

steps.title: Check CPR Feedback Pad/Device Connection
steps.instructions: Inspect the CPR feedback puck/sensor connection to the electrode pads. Ensure it is firmly seated with no loose or disconnected components. A loose connection will prevent feedback from functioning.

steps.title: Inspect Electrode Pads for Integrated Feedback Components
steps.instructions: Verify that the installed pads are the correct type with CPR feedback capability. Standard pads without feedback will not provide compression guidance.

steps.title: Check Pad Expiration Date
steps.instructions: Expired pads may disable or affect feedback features. Replace pads if expired or near expiration and retest.

steps.title: Inspect Cable and Connector Condition
steps.instructions: Check for bent pins, damaged connectors, or frayed cables between pads and AED. Damage can interrupt communication with the feedback system.

steps.title: Power Cycle the Device
steps.instructions: Remove the battery, wait 10 seconds, then reinstall. Observe for normal self-test behavior and listen for voice prompts related to CPR feedback.

steps.title: Observe Self-Test Indicators
steps.instructions: Check the Rescue Ready indicator and listen for any fault tones. A failed self-test may disable advanced features like CPR feedback.

steps.title: Perform a Functional Test (No Patient Connected)
steps.instructions: Install known-good CPR feedback pads and simulate a rescue scenario per facility protocol. Confirm whether CPR prompts and feedback activate. If feedback works → Issue resolved (likely pads or connection issue). If no feedback → Continue troubleshooting.

steps.title: Try Known Good Accessories
steps.instructions: Swap with a verified working set of CPR feedback pads/device. If resolved → Original accessory is faulty. If not resolved → Continue.

CCR.Complaint: CPR feedback not providing prompts during AED use.
CCR.Cause: Failed CPR feedback function not resolved after replacing pads and verifying connections.
CCR.Resolution: Removed AED from service and sent to vendor for evaluation and repair.

helpfulDetails: CPR feedback device present or not
Physical condition of connectors and cables
Final device status (in service / out of service)

title: Cardiac Science Powerheart G5 Defibrillator - Internal Memory or Data Download Errors
description: Addresses failures in event data storage, memory errors, or inability to export/download event logs to external software.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm the AED is not in active clinical use. Move the patient to another device if necessary before troubleshooting.

steps.title: Check Device Power
steps.instructions: Verify the battery is fully charged and seated correctly. Low power can cause memory write errors. Expected outcome: device powers on and self-tests without battery warnings.

steps.title: Inspect Memory/SD Card Access (if applicable)
steps.instructions: Check that any removable memory card or USB/data interface is properly seated, undamaged, and free of debris. Outcome: card recognized by device or software.

steps.title: Attempt Data Download via Manufacturer Software
steps.instructions: Connect the device to a known working computer using the recommended Cardiac Science software and cables. Outcome: event logs appear in the software.

steps.title: Verify Software/Driver Updates
steps.instructions: Confirm that the AED software and computer drivers are up to date. Outdated software can prevent proper communication. Expected outcome: device recognized, download initiated successfully.

steps.title: Test with Alternate Computer or Port
steps.instructions: Use a different USB port or computer to rule out local PC issues. Outcome: successful recognition and download of event data.

steps.title: Check for Device Error Messages or Logs
steps.instructions: Review AED screen for any error codes related to memory, storage, or communication. Outcome: note codes for documentation or escalation.

CCR.Complaint: AED failed to download event logs; memory errors displayed.
CCR.Cause: Memory access or data communication error identified during troubleshooting.
CCR.Resolution: Device removed from service and sent for bench evaluation; software attempted on alternate PC without success.

helpfulDetails: Attempts on multiple computers or ports
